content.text: AWS China is not part of the global AWS infrastructure. The Chinese government requires that cloud services operating within mainland China be provided by a licensed Chinese entity. AWS partners with two Chinese companies — Sinnet and NWCD — who hold the required ICP (Internet Content Provider) licenses and operate the AWS infrastructure in China on behalf of their customers.

attrs.alt: AWS China partition: cn-north-1 (Beijing, Sinnet) and cn-northwest-1 (Ningxia, NWCD) separated from commercial AWS

content.text: This partnership structure is unlike anything else in AWS's global footprint:
— the same hypervisors, S3 infrastructure, and APIs as global AWS.
(Beijing Sinnet Technology Co., Ltd.) operates cn-north-1 in Beijing. You sign a contract with Sinnet, not AWS. Sinnet handles billing and support in Chinese.
(Ningxia Western Cloud Data Technology Co., Ltd.) operates cn-northwest-1 in Ningxia. Same model as Sinnet.
— your global AWS account cannot be used in China. You create a new account with the Chinese operator.
(Chinese Yuan) and invoiced by the Chinese operator, subject to Chinese tax regulations.
Because Sinnet and NWCD are the licensees, they — not Amazon — are legally responsible for the cloud services they provide. AWS provides the technology stack under a licensing agreement.
AWS China uses the aws-cn partition, separate from the commercial aws partition and the US government aws-us-gov partition. This affects everything that encodes a partition in its string:
— a common mistake). An SDK configured for
will automatically resolve to the correct hostname.

The two regions are independent. You can replicate data between them (e.g., S3 cross-region replication, RDS read replicas) just like between any two global AWS regions, but all traffic stays within mainland China.
Any internet-facing service hosted on AWS China must have an ICP (Internet Content Provider) license issued by the Ministry of Industry and Information Technology (MIIT). This includes:
Websites accessible via a domain name registered in China (.cn domains, or .com domains used in China).
APIs exposed to users in China through CloudFront or directly.
Mobile app backends accessible from China over the public internet.

ICP filing (备案, Beian) is required for informational sites. ICP license (许可证) is required for commercial operations like e-commerce or interactive features. The distinction matters — filing takes weeks; a full license can take months.

AWS China verifies your ICP status before activating CloudFront distributions or enabling public internet-facing features. Internal services (accessible only via VPN or Direct Connect) typically do not require an ICP license.

# The ICP license number appears in website footers
# Example: 京ICP备12345678号 (Beijing ICP filing #12345678)
#           宁ICP备12345678号-1 (Ningxia ICP filing)

# In CloudFront distributions on AWS China, you provide your ICP number
# when creating the distribution — AWS verifies it before activation.
Setting up AWS China requires working with the Chinese operators directly:

— go to sinnet.com.cn (for Beijing) or nwcdcloud.cn (for Ningxia). Provide business license, ICP details, and contact information. Entity must be a Chinese legal entity or a foreign company with a Chinese subsidiary.
— the operator reviews your application and activates the account. This takes days to weeks.
— use a separate CLI profile with the China region and your China credentials.
— create separate IAM users, roles, and policies. China IAM is isolated from global IAM.

# ~/.aws/credentials
[china-sinnet]
aws_access_key_id     = AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE
aws_secret_access_key = wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY

# ~/.aws/config
[profile china-sinnet]
region = cn-north-1

# Use the profile
aws --profile china-sinnet ec2 describe-instances
aws --profile china-sinnet s3 ls
AWS China offers a broad set of services but the catalog is smaller than global AWS. Key services are available; some newer or niche services have not yet launched in China:
EC2, ECS, EKS, Fargate, Lambda (limited), Batch
S3, EBS (gp3, io2), EFS, S3 Glacier
No Route 53 public hosted zones (ICP constraint)
No Bedrock in cn-north-1 (only cn-northwest-1 limited)
IAM, KMS, CloudTrail, WAF, Shield Standard
No Shield Advanced, limited Security Hub
No AWS Marketplace (bring your own software)
The AWS China Service Endpoints documentation lists all available services and their endpoints. Check it before finalizing your architecture.
VPCs in AWS China work identically to global VPCs. Key networking considerations:

you cannot use Route 53 for public DNS in China. Domain resolution for public-facing services requires using a Chinese DNS provider (like Alibaba Cloud DNS or DNSPod). Route 53 private hosted zones work normally within your VPC.
available from Chinese carriers (China Telecom, China Unicom, China Mobile). BGP peering termination is in China, so latency to on-premises Chinese facilities is low.
Site-to-Site VPN is available. Note that China's regulations on encryption may affect VPN configurations — use approved cipher suites.

traffic to the public internet exits through the operator's infrastructure, which is subject to China's Great Firewall. Outbound API calls to global AWS services will not work from within an AWS China VPC.
you cannot VPC peer between a China VPC and a global AWS VPC. Use application-level replication or database sync for cross-region data transfer.

# Create VPC in China (same API as global)
aws --profile china-sinnet ec2 create-vpc \
  --cidr-block 10.0.0.0/16 \
  --tag-specifications 'ResourceType=vpc,Tags=[{Key=Name,Value=china-prod}]'

# Route 53 private hosted zone (works in China)
aws --profile china-sinnet route53 create-hosted-zone \
  --name internal.example.cn \
  --caller-reference unique-string-$(date +%s) \
  --hosted-zone-config PrivateZone=true \
  --vpc VPCRegion=cn-north-1,VPCId=vpc-0abc123

Multi-Level Protection Scheme (MLPS, 等级保护, Dengji Baohu) is China's mandatory information security framework, similar to FedRAMP in the US. Most commercial applications running in China require MLPS Level 2 or Level 3 certification:
Logging, access control, encryption at rest
Critical information infrastructure, financial systems, healthcare
Audit trails, intrusion detection, formal assessment by certified agency
Air-gapped networks, physical security — not applicable to most businesses

AWS China provides an MLPS compliance package for Level 2 and Level 3, mapping AWS services to the required controls. CloudTrail, CloudWatch Logs, KMS encryption, and Security Hub findings are commonly used to satisfy MLPS logging and encryption requirements.
The AWS Terraform provider supports AWS China by configuring the correct partition region:

Many multinational companies need their application to serve both global and Chinese users. Because AWS China is a separate partition, you cannot share IAM roles, VPC peering, or CloudFront distributions. The standard pattern uses application-level replication:
configure separate S3 buckets in both China and global, with scheduled sync (CRR does not work cross-partition; use AWS DataSync or custom scripts).
run separate RDS instances. Use Aurora global database if available, or custom MySQL/PostgreSQL replication. All cross-partition traffic goes over the public internet (consider encryption).
one distribution in global for international users, one in AWS China (ICP required) for Chinese users. Use GeoDNS at your domain registrar to route based on user location.
CodePipeline in global AWS cannot deploy to AWS China. Maintain parallel pipelines or use a third-party CI tool that supports both.
AWS Parameter Store and Secrets Manager are partition-isolated. Store configuration separately in each partition.
SDK configuration that defaults to global endpoints will fail silently — requests go to the wrong partition. Always set the region explicitly (
AWS Marketplace does not operate in AWS China. You must bring your own licenses for commercial software (SQL Server, Oracle, Windows, third-party security tools).
IAM policies and resource configurations with
will be invalid in China. Use partition-aware code.
building a public DNS dependency on Route 53 that works globally will fail in China — Chinese users need a Chinese DNS provider resolving your domain.
ICP filing or licensing can take 4–12 weeks. Starting the process after the code is ready delays go-live significantly.
launching commercial services in China without MLPS assessment exposes you to regulatory risk. Engage a certified MLPS assessment agency early.
Encryption and KMS in AWS China
AWS KMS is available in both China regions. KMS keys are scoped to the China partition and cannot be used to decrypt data in global AWS regions, and vice versa.
(e.g., aws/s3, aws/ebs) are automatically provisioned per service within the China partition.
can be created in each China region separately. A key in cn-north-1 cannot be used in cn-northwest-1 — create separate keys if you need both regions.
are available for KMS in China:
is available in AWS China for hardware-backed key storage, required by some MLPS Level 3 implementations.

# Create CMK in China
aws --profile china-sinnet kms create-key \
  --description 'China prod encryption key' \
  --key-usage ENCRYPT_DECRYPT \
  --region cn-north-1

# Encrypt data with the China CMK
aws --profile china-sinnet kms encrypt \
  --key-id arn:aws-cn:kms:cn-north-1:123456789012:key/abc-123 \
  --plaintext fileb://secret.txt \
  --query CiphertextBlob \
  --output text | base64 --decode > secret.enc
CloudFront is available in AWS China but with constraints that make it meaningfully different from global CloudFront:
— you must submit your ICP license number when creating a distribution. AWS China verifies it before the distribution goes live. Without an ICP license, you cannot use CloudFront in China.
— AWS China CloudFront has edge nodes in major Chinese cities (Beijing, Shanghai, Shenzhen, etc.) but fewer PoPs than the global network.
— CloudFront distributions in China are in the aws-cn partition. Distributions created in global AWS do not serve requests from within China.
— origins must be within China (typically S3 in cn-north-1 or an EC2-based origin). You cannot set a global S3 bucket as the origin for a China CloudFront distribution.
— use AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) in the China region for SSL certificates on your distribution. ACM is available in both China regions.
S3 in China uses the same APIs as global S3 but with different default behaviors driven by regulatory requirements:
Block Public Access is on by default
— AWS China requires all new S3 buckets to have public access blocked. Enabling public access requires explicit configuration and an ICP license for internet-facing content.
is not available in China regions.
— availability varies; check the current service page.
work identically. Transition objects to S3 Glacier for cold storage after a retention period.
works between cn-north-1 and cn-northwest-1 (within the aws-cn partition). Cross-partition replication (China to global) is not supported via native CRR — use custom scripting or AWS DataSync.
Amazon EKS is available in both China regions. EKS worker nodes run on EC2 instances in your VPC; the control plane is managed by AWS China (Sinnet or NWCD). Operational considerations:

— AWS ECR is available. For pulling public images (DockerHub, quay.io), internet connectivity from within China may be restricted by the Great Firewall. Mirror needed images to ECR in China before deploying.
— some EKS managed add-ons available globally may not be available in China. Check EKS China documentation for the supported add-on list.
— the AWS Load Balancer Controller works in China, but requires the China-specific IAM policy ARNs.
— available in China; Fargate profiles work the same way as in global regions.

# Create EKS cluster in China
aws --profile china-sinnet eks create-cluster \
  --name china-prod-cluster \
  --role-arn arn:aws-cn:iam::123456789012:role/EKSClusterRole \
  --resources-vpc-config \
    subnetIds=subnet-0abc,subnet-0def,\
    securityGroupIds=sg-0ghi \
  --kubernetes-version 1.29 \
  --region cn-north-1

# Update kubeconfig for China cluster
aws --profile china-sinnet eks update-kubeconfig \
  --region cn-north-1 \
  --name china-prod-cluster
CloudWatch and CloudTrail work in AWS China the same way as globally. For MLPS compliance, audit logging must be enabled and logs retained:
— enable multi-region trail covering both cn-north-1 and cn-northwest-1 if using both. Deliver logs to an S3 bucket with versioning and MFA-delete enabled.
— retention period must be set (MLPS typically requires 180 days minimum). Default is 'Never expire' (unlimited retention, costs accumulate).
— required for MLPS Level 3 network access auditing. Enable flow logs for all VPCs and deliver to CloudWatch Logs or S3.
— AWS Config is available in China for resource configuration compliance. Use managed Config rules to detect drift from your security baseline.

# Enable CloudTrail in China
aws --profile china-sinnet cloudtrail create-trail \
  --name china-audit-trail \
  --s3-bucket-name china-audit-logs-bucket \
  --is-multi-region-trail \
  --enable-log-file-validation \
  --region cn-north-1

aws --profile china-sinnet cloudtrail start-logging \
  --name china-audit-trail

# Enable VPC flow logs
aws --profile china-sinnet ec2 create-flow-logs \
  --resource-type VPC \
  --resource-ids vpc-0china123 \
  --traffic-type ALL \
  --log-destination-type cloud-watch-logs \
  --log-group-name /aws/vpc/flowlogs/china-prod \
  --deliver-logs-permission-arn arn:aws-cn:iam::123456789012:role/FlowLogsRole
AWS China pricing is independent of global AWS pricing and is denominated in CNY. General patterns:
Varies by instance type and operator
Within China the same VPC rules apply
Varies by engine and instance class

Billing goes through the Chinese operator (Sinnet or NWCD), not through your global AWS account. You receive a Chinese tax invoice (增值税发票, VAT invoice) which is required for Chinese corporate tax purposes. If you need consolidated billing across China and global, you must handle it at the accounting level — there is no AWS-native consolidated billing across partitions.

AWS China is a legally distinct operation — not owned by Amazon but licensed to Sinnet (Beijing, cn-north-1) and NWCD (Ningxia, cn-northwest-1). You create accounts with the operator, not with AWS directly.
The aws-cn partition is fully isolated from global AWS and GovCloud. No shared IAM, no VPC peering, no cross-partition ARNs. Endpoint hostnames end in .amazonaws.com.cn.
ICP license is mandatory for all public-facing services. Start the ICP application process before building your architecture.
Most major AWS services are available in China, but some newer services lag or are unavailable. Verify service availability before committing to an architecture.
MLPS Level 2 or 3 compliance is required for most commercial applications. CloudTrail, KMS, and Security Hub findings map to the key MLPS controls.
